ChangeRequest.name: [SHS] Add rank level for subordinated securities
ChangeRequest.comment:
<!-- See: documentation on [Issues in GitLab](https://gitlab.sofa.dev/sdd-bird-team/bird-logical-data-model/-/wikis/Issues-in-GitLab) -->
# Overview SHS needs the ranking of Securities and ABS's according to the table below: | Rank level | | | |------------|------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | X1X | Subordinated - Junior level | Subordinated securities have a lower priority than the issuer’s other securities | | X2X | Subordinated - Senior level | in the event of liquidation. A further differentiation can be made between | | X3X | Subordinated - No further breakdown available | Junior and Senior level with the Senior level having a higher priority. | | X4X | Senior | Senior securities are required to be repaid before subordinated securities. | | X5X | ABS Class - Junior | In case of Asset-backed securities, a breakdown of the tranch level is to be | | X6X | ABS Class - Mezzanine | provided. | | X7X | ABS Class - Senior | | | X9X | Rank level - No detailed information available | To be reported, in case the rank level is not available / applicable. |
Questions to answer: 1. Are the values for ABS and Subordinated mutually exclusive, or can a security classified as _ABS Class - Mezzanine_ also have the classification _Subordinated - Senior level_? 2. Is the seniority level we have at the ABS the same as what is meant here with ABS Class - Junior/mezzanine/senior? 3. Or do the ABS members apply only to subordinated debts? 4. Can the "Senior" value be applicable to subordinated debts, or only to non-subordinated debts? 5. If "Senior is only applicable to non-subordinated debts, are all non-subordinated debts always classified as "Senior"?
### Contact person @Arjan.Bos, @sdressler
### Legal definition SHS
### Reason for inclusion SHS
